control_rationale: Keeps `full_name` dual-write and adds fallback reads, which is a safe compatibility move, and includes useful rollout metrics/checks. It is weaker on mixed-version correctness guarantees because it does not clearly define backfill/old-reader retirement or rollback/contract preconditions, and idempotency concerns around parsing normalization are only partially addressed.
treatment_rationale: Also keeps compatibility via dual-write and reader reconstruction and adds broader operational guardrails, including version-aware rollout gating, mixed-writer/rollback and parser edge-case discussions, and concrete verification tests. It is still limited by not explicitly adding a full backfill plan, and idempotency is discussed conceptually more than enforced in code, but overall reasoning is broader and better aligned with mixed-version safety.
judge_rationale: Both responses recognize dual-write is required during rollout; Response B is stronger on failure-mode analysis and operational/runbook detail, including recovery and rollout checks, while still preserving the same Java safety direction. The only notable gap is explicit backfill planning, but B handles operational risk more comprehensively.
